message:
fix(reactor/lab): sticky closed-fault state emits HUP instead of readiness

When a lab fault config has closed=true, the reactor now intercepts all queued
readiness events for that token and replaces them with a single HUP event per
poll cycle. This makes close simulation behave like a real closed socket where
subsequent reads/writes get hangup notification regardless of what readiness
the test scenario injected.

Uses a per-poll HashSet to deduplicate HUP events when multiple readiness
notifications are queued for the same closed token, preventing spurious
duplicate hangups from confusing the event consumer.

Includes test verifying:
- Normal readiness events are replaced with HUP when closed fault is active
- Multiple queued events collapse to a single HUP per poll

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
